title: Ordered CatBoost categorical expert
summary: This dataset has very high-cardinality identities and sparse interactions across Driver, Race, Year, Compound, and Stint.
rationale: This dataset has very high-cardinality identities and sparse interactions across Driver, Race, Year, Compound, and Stint. CatBoost’s ordered categorical processing is designed to avoid leakage while building target-statistic combinations automatically, which the current manual frequency features may only approximate.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoostClassifier on raw categorical columns plus the best numeric strategy features. Keep categories un-one-hot-encoded, test a compact ordered-boosting setup first, and blend its OOF predictions with the existing tree model by rank or logistic stacking.
expected_effect: Most likely gain is on sparse entity interactions such as rare drivers, race-year shifts, and compound-by-stint behavior.
risk: CV can be slower and noisy if folds are not race-purged; gains may be small if current numeric heuristics already dominate signal.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
